checks.a_analysis: The output text corresponds to the source document. No invented or hallucinated paragraphs or sentences were found. AI-generated elements like the title and alt text are permitted exceptions.

URLs references found in the document (such as https://myncid.nc.gov/) were verified to be correct where they are explicitly present in the output. There is no evidence of fabricated or incorrect URLs. Instances of missing URLs in specific sections are categorized as text omissions.
All six pages of the source document are represented in the output JSON, including all primary headings and instruction sections.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. Information is presented in lists, which have been captured in list elements.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is significant text omission in the instructional steps on Pages 1 and 2. In the 'Email', 'Text Message', and 'Phone Call' sections on Page 1, the login URL (https://myncid.nc.gov/) and nearly all button labels (e.g., ADD ENROLLMENT, ADD, NEXT, SUBMIT) are consistently omitted from the text content of the list items. On Page 2, the 'Authenticator' section's second step similarly omits the login URL. These omissions account for more than 25% of the content in these specific instructional lists, resulting in incomplete and non-sensical instructions (e.g., 'click: ' with no target).
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 - Email Section: 1. Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/... 3. Click on: [ADD ENROLLMENT] 4. Select 'Email' and click: [ADD] 5. Verify the email address that pops up is correct and click: [NEXT] 6. Enter the 8 digit code that you received in your email and click: [SUBMIT]
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Login to " ... "text": "Click on: " ... "text": "Select “Email” and click: " ... "text": "Verify the email address that pops up is correct and click: " ... "text": "Enter the 8 digit code that you received in your email and click: "

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording of the captured text generally matches the source. Icons in Page 3 have been described by the AI (e.g., '[edit icon]', '[trash icon]'), which is an acceptable accessibility adjustment and does not alter the meaning.

checks.a_analysis: The output document preserves the repetitive header and title information found at the top of each page in the source PDF (e.g., 'MyNCID User Guide for MFA'). The image elements provided for the logos and UI buttons correspond to unique instances or intentional repetitions within the source document. No unintended duplications of paragraphs or sections were found.

The heading hierarchy is mostly preserved on pages 1-3, correctly moving from H1 to H2 to H3. However, on page 4, the sub-headings 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method' (which are visually equivalent to the H3 headings on page 1) are converted into list items within an unordered list (reading_order 60) directly under the H2. This eliminates the subsection hierarchy for that page and represents a major structural corruption.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual sub-headings: 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method' on Page 4.
checks.b_evidence_output: {"text": "For an existing MFA method"}, {"text": "For a new MFA method"} inside a 'list' element.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; the content is organized via headings and lists. Therefore, no table structure corruption is present.

On page 4, the entire procedural instruction text (steps 1-5 for both the existing and new MFA methods) is missing from the converted output. The reading order jumps from the converted sub-headers directly to image crops, omitting ten major steps of text content found in the source. This is a material divergence from the source's intended information flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: Steps 1-5 for 'For an existing MFA method' and Steps 1-5 for 'For a new MFA method' on Page 4.
checks.b_evidence_output: [Content entirely absent from JSON output after the list at reading_order 60]

checks.a_analysis: The procedural steps throughout the document are presented as numbered (ordered) lists in the source PDF. The output converts all of these into 'unordered' lists, losing the sequential meaning. Additionally, on page 4, the structural layout of headings and subsequent steps is collapsed into a simple two-item unordered list containing only the sub-headers.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered procedural steps (e.g., 1. Login to..., 2. Click on..., etc.) throughout the document.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document (e.g., sections[0], sections[2], sections[4])

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' is clearly visible and clickable in the source PDF on every page. In the output, for pages 1 and 2 (sections[0] and sections[1]), the URL is entirely missing from the text strings (e.g., 'Login to '). While the text is present on later pages, the conversion fails to create a functional link object for these government URLs.
checks.b_evidence_source: Step 1 on Page 1: '1. Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/'

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly linearizes the multi-column layout of the source PDF. Each page's content is grouped logically and follows the flow of the original document. While the layout changed from columns to a single-column scroll, this is expected in responsive HTML conversion and does not hinder the visual organization or finding of information.
No tables were found in the source PDF or the converted HTML output. The content consists primarily of instructional lists and headings.

Several meaningful UI icons and graphics present in the source PDF are missing from the HTML output or have been replaced by text placeholders. On page 3, the 'edit' icon is missing and replaced with text. On page 4, the radio button ('Set as default') and checkbox ('Make as default') graphics are completely missing. On page 5, the 'trash' icon is missing and replaced with text. These are meaningful instructional images referred to in the text steps.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF Page 3 shows pencil 'edit' icons. Page 4 shows a radio button graphic for 'Set as default' and a checkbox graphic for 'Make as default'. Page 5 shows a 'trash' icon.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML output replaces edit icons with '[edit icon]' text, replaces trash icon with '[trash icon]' text, and completely omits the radio button and checkbox graphics under 'Change default MFA enrollment method'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Modify MFA enrollment (Page 3), Change default MFA enrollment method (Page 4), Delete MFA method (Page 5)

checks.a_analysis: No genuine body content (paragraphs, tables, or instructional steps) is duplicated in the HTML output. The repetition of the header logo corresponds to its appearance on every page of the original PDF, which is acceptable.
